creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Add a global --interactive / -i flag that walks the user through the args of a script interactively, then prints the equivalent non-interactive invocation (to stderr) and runs it. One safe, user-initiated entry point that subsumes the "pick an enum value" and "pick a command" ideas, and doubles as an invocation-builder.

STATUS: design-only. BLOCKED on replacing huh.

== Dependency / blocker ==
All prompting (pick/multipick/confirm/input) calls charmbracelet/huh directly and is effectively untestable today: snapshot tests only hit the single-match early-return, and huh reads the OS TTY directly, bypassing the injectable RIo.StdIn seam. This feature must be snapshot-testable, so it is gated on replacing huh behind a small injectable Prompter interface (Select / MultiSelect / Confirm / Input), wired like the existing RShell / RReq / RClock injection in core/global.go + core/testing/test_helpers.go. Retrofitting the existing pick/input/confirm builtins onto Prompter closes their test gap too.

== Architecture (decided) ==
Interactive mode is a pre-pass that synthesizes the missing argv, then hands off to the existing Ra parse. Ra stays the single source of truth for validation, and the equivalent-invocation output is just the argv we built. Hook point: core/runner.go parseAndExecute, after the first parse (where -i, which-args-supplied, and which-command-matched are known) and before ResetParseState / the final parse.

== Behavior (decided) ==
- TTY required; clear error if not a terminal.
- If command: blocks exist and none given, Select the command first, then prompt that command args + shared script args.
- Prompt for every arg NOT already supplied on the CLI (so -i composes with partial CLI args); optional args skippable (Enter = default/null).
- Type mapping: enum str -> Select; bool -> Confirm; int/float -> Input (+ range check); free str -> Input (regex can validate). Lists / variadic NEED DESIGN.
- Show each arg # comment as the prompt; show default as placeholder.
- Print equivalent invocation (external/hyphenated names, quoted as needed) to stderr, then run.

== Hard part (open) ==
Relational constraints. Prompt-for-all collides with requires/excludes (and mutual). Leading approach: constraint-aware reactive prompting (track chosen/excluded as we walk; A excludes B -> skip B; A requires C -> force C), plus a "which of these do you want to supply?" Select for mutually-exclusive groups. Final Ra parse is the backstop. Exact UX TBD at implementation.

== Implementation sketch ==
1. (prereq, separate card) Replace huh + add Prompter seam.
2. Add FLAG_INTERACTIVE / FLAG_I + FlagInteractive in core/flags_global.go; register in CreateAndRegisterGlobalFlags; reset in ResetGlobals. (-i is free.)
3. Interactive pre-pass in parseAndExecute, likely a new core/interactive.go (testable in isolation: metadata + scripted Prompter -> synthesized argv).
4. Confirm Ra API for per-flag was-set introspection (usedPtr covers commands); may need a small upstream ra addition.
5. Snapshot tests via a new scripted-prompt section: enum/bool/int/free-str, command pick + cascade, partial args + -i, non-TTY error, equivalent-invocation stderr.

== Out of scope (future cards) ==
- Enum auto-pick when omitted WITHOUT -i (changes default contract; foot-gun).
- Per-arg author opt-in syntax (touches args syntax; needs its own RED doc).
- @enable_interactive script-level macro (mirrors @enable_args_block).
- Build-only variant (print invocation, do not run).

Promote this design to a docs/red/ RED doc when we commit to building.
title: Interactive arg-entry mode (--interactive / -i)
